source: AD&D 2nd Edition Player's Handbook, Dungeon Master's Guide, Arms & Equipment Guide
description: Complete adventuring equipment, tools, clothing, mounts, vehicles, and services with prices in gold pieces

container_and_carrying.name: Spell component pouch
container_and_carrying.notes: Belt-worn pouch with multiple compartments for spell components

container_and_carrying.name: Haversack
container_and_carrying.notes: Over-shoulder bag, easier access than backpack

light_sources.name: Lantern (hooded)
light_sources.notes: Can be hooded to direct light, conceals light in one direction

light_sources.name: Lantern (bullseye)
light_sources.notes: Projects beam in one direction, 60-foot cone

light_sources.name: Oil (lamp), per pint
light_sources.notes: Fuel for lanterns, also useful as a weapon (burning oil)

light_sources.name: Oil (Greek fire), per pint
light_sources.notes: Highly flammable oil for burning oil attacks, 1d6+1 rounds

light_sources.name: Torch
light_sources.notes: Wooden torch soaked in pitch; can be used as weapon (1d4 dmg)

light_sources.name: Tinder box
light_sources.notes: Flint, steel, and tinder to start fires; takes 1d4 rounds to light a fire

light_sources.name: Signal flare
light_sources.notes: Burns for 1 minute, visible 1 mile away, comes in colors

rope_and_climbing.name: Rope (silk), 50 ft
rope_and_climbing.notes: Lighter and stronger per weight than hemp

rope_and_climbing.name: Grappling hook
rope_and_climbing.notes: 4-pronged hook for rope throwing; +2 to climbing checks

rope_and_climbing.name: Pitons (12)
rope_and_climbing.notes: Iron spikes for rock climbing; require hammer

rope_and_climbing.name: Pulley (block and tackle)
rope_and_climbing.notes: Multiplies lifting force by 4 (reduces effective weight)

rope_and_climbing.name: Crowbar
rope_and_climbing.notes: Iron pry bar; +2 to open door/crate checks

tools_and_equipment.name: Acid (flask)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Deals 1d6 damage on contact; may dissolve locks or bars over time

tools_and_equipment.name: Caltrops (1 lb bag)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Scatters over 5 sq ft; 1d4+1 damage to barefoot or lightly shod; move at half speed

tools_and_equipment.name: Chain (per foot)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Iron chain; breaking strength depends on links

tools_and_equipment.name: Chalk (10 pieces)
tools_and_equipment.notes: For marking dungeon walls, writing

tools_and_equipment.name: Fishing net (10 sq ft)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Can be used to entangle creatures

tools_and_equipment.name: Glass cutter
tools_and_equipment.notes: Cuts glass panes silently

tools_and_equipment.name: Glue (flask)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Strong adhesive; 1 hour drying time; 250 lb holding strength

tools_and_equipment.name: Magnifying glass
tools_and_equipment.notes: Can start fires in direct sunlight; aids reading fine print

tools_and_equipment.name: Manacles (iron)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Restrains humanoid wrists; key required; STR 18 to break

tools_and_equipment.name: Manacles (silver)
tools_and_equipment.notes: For creatures affected by silver; same stats as iron

tools_and_equipment.name: Mirror (small, steel)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Useful to look around corners or check for medusa; reflects gaze attacks

tools_and_equipment.name: Mirror (large, steel)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Larger version, better for gaze attacks

tools_and_equipment.name: Mirror (silver)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Effective against vampires per some traditions

tools_and_equipment.name: Needle and thread
tools_and_equipment.notes: Basic sewing; can suture wounds (First Aid proficiency)

tools_and_equipment.name: Oil of acid resistance
tools_and_equipment.notes: Protects metal from acid for 1 hour

tools_and_equipment.name: Spike (iron)
tools_and_equipment.notes: For jamming doors or securing ropes

tools_and_equipment.name: Stakes (3, wooden)
tools_and_equipment.notes: 3 wooden stakes; useful against vampires

tools_and_equipment.name: Thieves' tools (basic)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Picks, tension bars, probes; required for Open Locks and Remove Traps skills

tools_and_equipment.name: Thieves' tools (quality)
tools_and_equipment.notes: +5% to Open Locks and Remove Traps checks

tools_and_equipment.name: Whetstone
tools_and_equipment.notes: For sharpening blades; restores minor dulling

tools_and_equipment.name: Wire (10 ft)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Thin iron wire for traps or mechanical uses

tools_and_equipment.name: Wolfsbane (bunch)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Repels werewolves and lycanthropes if thrust at them (ST vs. poison or flee)

tools_and_equipment.name: Holy water (vial)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Deals 2d4 damage to undead and evil outsiders; blessed by cleric

tools_and_equipment.name: Holy symbol (wooden)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Required for clerical turning and spells

tools_and_equipment.name: Holy symbol (silver)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Preferred version for clerical use

tools_and_equipment.name: Holy symbol (gold)
tools_and_equipment.notes: High quality holy symbol

tools_and_equipment.name: Unholy water (vial)
tools_and_equipment.notes: As holy water but for good-aligned creatures

tools_and_equipment.name: Prayer beads
tools_and_equipment.notes: Religious item for meditation and prayer

tools_and_equipment.name: Periscope (small)
tools_and_equipment.notes: Mirror device to see over obstacles without exposing self

measurement_and_navigation.name: Astrolabe
measurement_and_navigation.notes: Navigation device; determines latitude; requires Navigation proficiency

measurement_and_navigation.name: Compass
measurement_and_navigation.notes: Points north; +2 to Navigation checks in wilderness

measurement_and_navigation.name: Ink (per oz)
measurement_and_navigation.notes: Standard black ink; colored inks cost more

measurement_and_navigation.name: Scale (merchants)
measurement_and_navigation.notes: Accurate to 1/10 oz; for weighing coins and gems

measurement_and_navigation.name: Spyglass
measurement_and_navigation.notes: Magnifies distant objects x2; useful for scouting

food_and_survival.name: Iron rations (per week)
food_and_survival.notes: Preserved food (salt pork, hardtack, dried fruit); 7 days of meals

food_and_survival.name: Standard rations (per week)
food_and_survival.notes: Fresh/semi-fresh food; 7 days of meals; spoils faster

food_and_survival.name: Spirits (per pint)
food_and_survival.notes: Distilled liquor; stronger than ale or wine

food_and_survival.name: Cooking pot
food_and_survival.notes: Iron pot for camp cooking; hangs over fire

food_and_survival.name: Mess kit
food_and_survival.notes: Bowl, spoon, fork, cup; tin or iron

food_and_survival.name: Hunting trap (large)
food_and_survival.notes: Bear trap; 2d4 damage, holds medium creatures

medical_and_alchemical.name: Antitoxin (vial)
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Counters one dose of non-magical poison if administered within 1 round

medical_and_alchemical.name: Healing herbs (packet)
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Various healing herbs; can be used with Herbalism proficiency

medical_and_alchemical.name: Healing salve
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Treats wounds; heals 1 HP over 8 hours of rest when applied

medical_and_alchemical.name: Surgical tools
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Required for surgery procedures; scalpels, needles, etc.

medical_and_alchemical.name: Alchemist's fire (flask)
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Burns for 1d4 rounds on contact; 1d6 damage per round; water doesn't extinguish

medical_and_alchemical.name: Smoke bomb
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Creates 10-ft radius smoke cloud for 1d4+2 rounds; obscures vision

medical_and_alchemical.name: Tanglefoot bag
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Bursts on impact; sticky material; target must save vs. paralysis or be entangled for 1d4 rounds

medical_and_alchemical.name: Thunderstone
medical_and_alchemical.notes: Deafens in 10-ft radius for 1d4 rounds on impact (save vs. breath weapon negates)

clothing_and_personal.name: Boots (leather, soft)
clothing_and_personal.notes: Standard footwear; soft-soled for quiet movement

clothing_and_personal.name: Cloak (common wool)
clothing_and_personal.notes: Basic wool cloak; protects from weather

clothing_and_personal.name: Surcoat
clothing_and_personal.notes: Tabard worn over armor for identification

clothing_and_personal.name: Traveling clothes
clothing_and_personal.notes: Complete outfit for travel: sturdy and practical

clothing_and_personal.name: Noble clothing
clothing_and_personal.notes: Fine courtly attire; required for high society interactions

clothing_and_personal.name: Disguise kit
clothing_and_personal.notes: Makeup, wigs, prosthetics; used with Disguise proficiency; provides up to 3 disguises

musical_instruments.name: Drum (war)
musical_instruments.notes: Military drum, loud; used to signal troops

musical_instruments.name: Horn (hunting)
musical_instruments.notes: Animal horn; signal use; carries up to 1 mile

musical_instruments.name: Bagpipes
musical_instruments.notes: Wind/reed instrument; very loud; associated with northern cultures

writing_and_scholarly.name: Spellbook (blank)
writing_and_scholarly.notes: 100 pages; quality binding; required for wizards; holds approx. 100 spell levels

writing_and_scholarly.name: Spellbook (traveling)
writing_and_scholarly.notes: 50 pages; lighter version for field use

writing_and_scholarly.name: Reference book (general)
writing_and_scholarly.notes: Written work on a subject; +1 to relevant knowledge checks

writing_and_scholarly.name: Map (world)
writing_and_scholarly.notes: Rare world map; may be inaccurate in distant regions

horses.name: Warhorse (heavy)
horses.notes: Trained for combat; attacks allowed; must be combat-trained; AC 7, HD 3+3

horses.name: Draft horse
horses.notes: Heavy working horse; pulls wagons; not trained for riding under combat

horses.name: Riding horse (medium)
horses.notes: Standard riding horse; flees from combat if not combat-trained

horses.name: Riding horse (light)
horses.notes: Fast light horse for messengers; AC 7, HD 1+1

horses.name: Pony
horses.notes: Smaller than horse; preferred by dwarves, gnomes, halflings

other_mounts.name: Camel
other_mounts.notes: Desert mount; 2-3 weeks without water; can go 3 days without food; surly temperament

other_mounts.name: Donkey/Mule
other_mounts.notes: Hardy pack animal; sure-footed on rough terrain; cannot be ridden in combat

other_mounts.name: Elephant
other_mounts.notes: Exotic beast of burden and war mount; requires special handling; 3d8 HD

other_mounts.name: Ox
other_mounts.notes: Slow but powerful draft animal; better than horse for heavy pulling

other_mounts.name: Riding dog (large)
other_mounts.notes: Can be ridden by halflings, gnomes; AC 7, HD 1+2

saddles_and_tack.name: Saddle (military)
saddles_and_tack.notes: Full military saddle with deep seat; +2 to riding checks while mounted in combat

saddles_and_tack.name: Saddle (pack)
saddles_and_tack.notes: Frame saddle for carrying loads rather than rider

saddles_and_tack.name: Halter
saddles_and_tack.notes: For leading horses; not for riding

saddles_and_tack.name: Horseshoes (set of 4)
saddles_and_tack.notes: Iron horseshoes; farrier required to fit

saddles_and_tack.name: Feed (per day)
saddles_and_tack.notes: Grain and hay for horse; required daily

saddles_and_tack.name: Stabling (per night)
saddles_and_tack.notes: Lodging for horse; includes feed and care

land_vehicles.name: Cart (one-horse)
land_vehicles.notes: Simple two-wheeled cart; requires one horse

land_vehicles.name: Chariot (war)
land_vehicles.notes: Two-wheeled war vehicle; 2 horses; archer or warrior can attack from it

land_vehicles.name: Carriage (royal/noble)
land_vehicles.notes: Ornate enclosed carriage; up to 4 horses

water_vehicles.name: Raft (small)
water_vehicles.notes: Logs lashed together; 10x10 ft; no shelter

water_vehicles.name: Canoe
water_vehicles.notes: Light paddled boat; can be carried

water_vehicles.name: Sailing ship (small)
water_vehicles.notes: 80-ft merchant vessel; crew 10-20; can cross oceans

lodging.name: Inn (common room, per night)
lodging.notes: Sleeping on the floor with other travelers

lodging.name: Inn (per room, per night)
lodging.notes: Shared room for up to 4 people

professional_services.name: Hireling (soldier, 0-level, heavy)
professional_services.notes: Heavy infantryman with chain and weapons

professional_services.name: Hireling (heavy cavalry)
professional_services.notes: Mounted heavy soldier; horse and armor included

professional_services.name: Sage (basic question)
professional_services.notes: Answers one simple question about a specialty area

professional_services.name: Sage (complex research, per day)
professional_services.notes: Research a complex topic; 1d6 days minimum

professional_services.name: Scribe (per page)
professional_services.notes: Copies documents; illuminated manuscripts cost more

professional_services.name: Weaponsmith
professional_services.notes: 1 gp/day labor plus materials for crafting weapons

professional_services.name: Thief-Catcher
professional_services.notes: Tracks and returns escaped prisoners; per job

professional_services.name: Assassin
professional_services.notes: Professional killer; highly illegal; 100+ gp per target depending on difficulty

religious_services.name: Resurrection
religious_services.notes: Full body resurrection; rare temples only

alchemical_substances.name: Acid (strong, flask)
alchemical_substances.notes: Deals 1d6 damage per round of contact; dissolves most metals over time

alchemical_substances.name: Alchemist's fire
alchemical_substances.notes: Ignites on contact with air; burns for 1d4 rounds; 1d6 damage/round; water spreads it

alchemical_substances.name: Antitoxin
alchemical_substances.notes: Neutralizes one dose of poison if taken within 1 round of poisoning

alchemical_substances.name: Flash powder (pinch)
alchemical_substances.notes: Blinding flash when ignited; all within 10 ft save vs. spell or blind 1 round

alchemical_substances.name: Noxious dust (packet)
alchemical_substances.notes: Inhaled irritant; 10-ft cloud; save vs. poison or sneeze/cough (-2 to attacks) for 1d4 rounds

alchemical_substances.name: Slow fire compound
alchemical_substances.notes: Treated to burn slowly; 4 hours per pound

alchemical_substances.name: Smoke powder
alchemical_substances.notes: Burns to create 10-ft smoke cloud, 2 rounds

alchemical_substances.name: Sovereign glue (oz)
alchemical_substances.notes: Extreme adhesive; bonds anything to anything in 1 round; only universal solvent breaks it

alchemical_substances.name: Universal solvent (oz)
alchemical_substances.notes: Dissolves any adhesive including sovereign glue

alchemical_substances.name: Itching powder
alchemical_substances.notes: Causes severe itching; -2 to all actions for 1d6 rounds if exposed

alchemical_substances.name: Oil of slipperiness
alchemical_substances.notes: Covers 10 sq ft; anything on it slides; DEX check to stand or fall prone

traps_and_alarms.name: Alarm trap (bell)
traps_and_alarms.notes: Wire-triggered bell alarm; detects passage through area

traps_and_alarms.name: Caltrops (iron, handful)
traps_and_alarms.notes: Scattered over 5 sq ft; 1d4+1 damage to unshod feet; half move until removed

traps_and_alarms.name: Noise trap
traps_and_alarms.notes: Small mechanical trap that creates loud noise when triggered

traps_and_alarms.name: Snare (rope)
traps_and_alarms.notes: Loop trap; trips or holds small to medium creatures

traps_and_alarms.name: Spring snare
traps_and_alarms.notes: Weighted snare; hoists small creatures off ground

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Iron spike (10)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Drive into stone/wood walls to jam doors, anchor ropes

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Wooden wedge (6)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Jam under doors to prevent them opening

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Beeswax (block)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Useful for making molds of keys, sealing containers

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Bucket
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Leather or wooden bucket; 1 gallon capacity

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Twine (100 ft)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Thin cord for marking passages, simple traps

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Sand (small bag)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Used to smother fires; traction on ice

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Sulfur (small bag)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Repels some creatures; component for fire spells

dungeoneering_supplies.name: Garlic (string)
dungeoneering_supplies.notes: Repels vampires; many undead dislike it

gemstones.examples: Azurite, banded agate, blue quartz, eye agate, hematite, lapis lazuli, malachite, moss agate, obsidian, rhodochrosite, tiger eye, turquoise
Bloodstone, carnelian, chalcedony, chrysoprase, citrine, iolite, jasper, moonstone, onyx, rock crystal, rose quartz, sardonyx, smoky quartz, star rose quartz, zircon
Alexandrite, amber, amethyst, aquamarine, chrysoberyl, coral, garnet, jade, jet, pearl (white), spinel, topaz, tourmaline
Black pearl, diamond (small), emerald, jacinth, opal, ruby (small), sapphire (large blue), star ruby, star sapphire
Black sapphire, diamond (large), jacinth (large), oriental amethyst/topaz, ruby (large), star sapphire (black)

art_objects.examples: Candelabra, carved bone, copper goblet, painted wood
Silver candlestick, carved ivory, bronze statuette, woven tapestry
Gold inlaid box, silver tiara, oil painting, decorated shield
Elven artwork, dragon-ivory carving, masterwork tapestry
Crown of a king, ancient masterwork, artifact-level craftsmanship
